item.text: I’ll verify this strictly through `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*` shell commands and identify only crates whose own `Cargo.toml` declares `orbit-knowledge` in dependencies/dev/build dependencies. I’m starting by discovering the available graph commands and output format.

item.aggregated_output:
Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

Usage: orbit tool [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list      List all registered tools
  show      Show detailed information about a tool
  run       Execute a tool
  add       Register an external tool or MCP plugin
  scaffold  Generate a starter external tool plugin
  remove    Remove an external tool
  enable    Enable a disabled tool
  disable   Disable a tool
  doctor    Validate tool health

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

Examples:
  orbit tool scaffold ./plugins/hello_orbit.py --name demo.hello
  orbit tool add ./plugins/hello_orbit.py
  orbit tool show demo.hello

item.text: I confirmed the CLI surface and next I’m enumerating registered graph tools so we can query dependencies without opening files directly.

error: store error: attempt to write a readonly database

item.text: I hit a runtime constraint: `orbit tool show` failed because the Orbit store wants to write to SQLite in this read-only environment. I’m checking for a supported read-only flag or alternate store location so we can still query graph data via shell.

item.aggregated_output:
orbit 0.1.0

Usage: orbit [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Setup:
  init       Initialize the global Orbit root (~/.orbit)
  workspace  Initialize and manage workspaces
  mcp        Manage MCP client integrations
  config     Show or update Orbit configuration

Resources:
  task       Create, update, and manage tasks
  activity   Define, list, and run activities
  job        Define, list, and manage job workflows
  policy     Manage filesystem profile policies and runtime scoping
  executor   Manage executors
  tool       Manage tools and external MCP plugins

Workflows:
  run        Run a job workflow (supports run ship / run duel / run job / run <id>)

Inspect:
  audit      Query the audit event log
  metrics    Inspect token, tool-call, and knowledge-pack metrics
  scoreboard Generate read-only scoreboard summaries
  graph      Query the knowledge graph

Serve:
  serve      Serve Orbit outward (serve web / serve mcp)

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help
  -V, --version      Print version

Build and query the knowledge graph

Usage: orbit graph [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  build   Build the knowledge graph from scratch
  update  Incrementally update the knowledge graph
  show    Show a node and its context
  search  Search nodes by name or location

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

Search nodes by name or location

Usage: orbit graph search [OPTIONS] <QUERY>

Arguments:
  <QUERY>  Search query (matches name or location)

Options:
      --root <ROOT>      Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --type <TYPE>      Filter by node type (dir, file, symbol); can be repeated
      --prefix <PREFIX>  Filter by location prefix
      --limit <LIMIT>    Max results [default: 20]
      --json             Output as JSON
      --ref <REF_NAME>   Knowledge-graph ref name (defaults to the current git branch)
  -h, --help             Print help

item.text: The graph dependency query succeeded and already shows which crates point to `orbit-knowledge`. I’ll now verify each consumer’s exact `Cargo.toml` path using graph search/show so the final list includes validated paths.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --full | jq -r '.crates | to_entries[] | select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\")) | .key'"
/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --full | jq -r '.crates | to_entries[] | select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\")) | .key'"

/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.search --input '{\"query\":\"Cargo.toml\",\"type\":\"file\",\"limit\":200}' --full | jq -r '.results[] | select(.file == \"crates/orbit-cli/Cargo.toml\" or .file == \"crates/orbit-tools/Cargo.toml\") | .file'"

/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.search --input '{\"query\":\"Cargo.toml\",\"type\":\"file\",\"limit\":200}' --full | jq -r '.results[] | select(.file == \"crates/orbit-cli/Cargo.toml\" or .file == \"crates/orbit-tools/Cargo.toml\") | .file'"

item.text: I’ve narrowed the direct dependents to two crates from `orbit.graph.deps`, and I’m running one final check to compute the exact count from the same graph output before I return the list.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --full | jq '[.crates | to_entries[] | select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\"))] | length'"
/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --full | jq '[.crates | to_entries[] | select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\"))] | length'"
